A.label: Carry his final message to the camp

steps.dialogue: Traveller. Please. I cannot rise. My horse bolted in the night when the wolves came and I took a blow meant for another man who fled without a word of thanks. I do not ask you to save me. I ask only that you carry a letter to the pilgrim camp beyond the pine ridge. My daughter waits there. Tell her that her father kept his oath to the last.

steps.description: Take the knight's sealed letter and his signet ring as proof of your errand.
Follow the highland trail eastward through dense pine groves toward the pilgrim camp. The path is narrow and the light fades quickly.
Search the pilgrim camp for the knight's daughter among the shelters and cookfires.

steps.dialogue: His ring. I know this ring. He wore it the day he left us to ride north with the vanguard. All these months I feared the worst and prayed against it. Thank you for bringing me his words. I will carry them the rest of the way to Grailsend. He would have wanted that. Please, take this. He would want his courage to travel onward even if he cannot.

Traveller. Please. I cannot rise. My horse bolted in the night when the wolves came and I took a blow meant for another man who fled without a word of thanks. I do not ask you to save me. I ask only that you carry a letter to the pilgrim camp beyond the pine ridge. My daughter waits there. Tell her that her father kept his oath to the last.

steps.description: Examine the knight's wounds. The gash along his side is deep but clean. With the right herbs and steady hands, he might yet survive.
Search the surrounding hillside for wild comfrey and yarrow to make a poultice for the knight's wounds.
Keep the campfire burning through the night as wolves circle closer, drawn by the scent of blood. Protect the wounded knight until dawn.

steps.dialogue: You stayed. By all the saints, you actually stayed. Most would have taken the letter and counted themselves virtuous. I owe you a debt I may never repay. When I can walk again I will find my daughter myself and tell her that good people still walk the King's Road. Take my shield. It has saved my life more times than I deserve.
